location.office_time_required: occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager

level_signals: 5+ years of proven experience in strategy, operations, or program management
Collaborates with senior leaders across InfoSec and the company
Comp range $129K-$152K base consistent with Senior IC at Airbnb

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Business, or a related field;
5+ years of proven experience in strategy, operations, or program management within a technology or engineering organization.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to quickly assess situations, identify root causes, and develop effective solutions.
Demonstrated excellence in communication and presentation, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels.
Demonstrated experience leading and influencing complex, cross-functional programs and projects from conception to completion.
Experience collaborating with security/software engineers to identify risks, think strategically, and translate high-level requirements into tactical plans.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with data analytics and visualization tools.
(Bonus) Background in Security organizations, with an understanding of security concepts and a broad range of risks and controls.
(Bonus) Familiarity with AI-enabled workflows (automation, summarization/synthesis, risk identification)

scope.surface: Airbnb Information Security organization — operational initiatives, planning, and efficiency improvements
primary_metrics_inferred: OKR achievement rate for InfoSec strategic initiatives
Operational efficiency and workflow automation adoption
core_responsibilities: Facilitate biannual planning cycles, including goal setting, cross-functional alignment, resource allocation, and budget management
Establish and track Objective Key Results (OKRs) to measure the effectiveness of strategic initiatives and operational processes
Drive the execution of major organizational projects, ensuring timely delivery and achievement of outcomes
Streamline workflows to improve efficiency, productivity, and scalability within InfoSec
Prepare company-wide InfoSec communications, including reports and emails for engineering audiences
Partner with organizational leaders on budgeting, headcount planning, and resource allocation
Provide actionable insights to support decision-making and assess the effectiveness of executive operations
Develop and implement best practices for project management, data analysis, and reporting

compensation.equity_form: eligible for bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($129K-$152K base) with bonus and equity eligibility
Clear scope and day-to-day responsibilities listed
Bonus qualifications clearly separated from required qualifications
Inclusive hiring language and disability accommodation process provided
red_flags: Must reside in a state where Airbnb has a registered entity — excluded states list not enumerated in JD, adds uncertainty for remote candidates
Visa sponsorship status not explicitly stated
JD does not specify whether security certifications (CISSP, CISM, etc.) are valued, leaving credential bar ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years of experience requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from Airbnb's known marketplace model)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred from US-state registration requirement; sponsorship not explicitly addressed)
ambiguities: Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; work_auth set to 'no-sponsorship' based on the US-state residency requirement language, but this is an inference.

The JD title span note ('Our job titles may span more than one career level') suggests this could be mapped to more than one internal level; seniority treated as Senior IC based on comp and experience signals.

Data analytics and visualization tools are listed under 'Preferred Qualifications' without a bonus label, while security background and AI workflows are explicitly labeled '(Bonus)' — treated all three as nice_to_haves but the first may be closer to a soft hard requirement.
